title: Metal boride aerogels
abstract: According to one inventive concept, a metal boride aerogel includes a three dimensional aerogel structure comprising metal boride particles having an average diameter of less than one micron.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A metal boride aerogel, comprising: a three dimensional aerogel structure comprising metal boride particles having an average diameter of less than one micron. 2. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1 , wherein the metal boride particles include particles having a metal boride selected from the group consisting of: hafnium boride, zirconium boride, and titanium boride. 3. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1 , wherein the three dimensional aerogel structure includes at least one metal selected from the group consisting of: hafnium, zirconium, titanium, vanadium, molybdenum, tantalum, niobium, chromium, tungsten, iron, cobalt, nickel, lanthanum, and uranium. 4. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1 , wherein the metal boride aerogel has, at least in part, a composition of MBj t Cy(Â¾, wherein x is a number in a range of greater than zero and less than ten, wherein y is a number in a range of greater than zero and less than ten, 

 wherein z is a number in a range of greater than or equal to zero and less than ten. 5. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1, wherein the metal boride particles have an average diameter of less than about 100 nanometers. 6. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1, wherein the metal boride aerogel is electrically insulating. 7. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1, wherein the metal boride aerogel has an electrical conductivity of about 1 Siemens per centimeter or less. 8. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1, having a surface area in a range of about 50 mg/cm 3 to about 1,000 mg/cm 3 . 9. The metal boride aerogel as recited in claim 1, wherein the metal boride aerogel is essentially free of metal oxide. 10. A method for forming a metal boride aerogel, the method comprising: dispersing boron nanoparticles in a solution of a metal salt; forming a boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel using the dispersed boron nanoparticles in the solution of the metal salt; drying the boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel; and 

 heating the dried boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel to form a metal boron aerogel, wherein the metal boron aerogel is essentially free of metal oxide. 11. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein the metal salt includes at least one metal selected from the group consisting of: hafnium, zirconium, titanium, vanadium, niobium, tantalum, chromium, molybdenum, tungsten, iron, cobalt, nickel, lanthanum, germanium, yttrium, manganese, and combinations thereof. 12. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein the metal salt is selected from the group consisting of: metal chloride, metal nitride, and metal alkoxide. 13. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein an average diameter of the boron nanoparticles is in a range of greater than zero and less than ten microns. 14. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein an average diameter of the boron nanoparticles is in a range of greater than zero and less than 100 nanometers. 15. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein forming the boron@metal oxide precursor aerogel comprises an epoxide-assisted sol gel process. 16. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the epoxide is selected from the group consisting of: propylene oxide, trimethylene oxide, dimethylene oxide, and ethylene oxide. 17. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein a duration of time of forming boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel is greater than zero minutes and less than ten minutes. 18. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein the drying comprises supercritical carbon dioxide drying. 19. The method as recited in claim 18, the boron@metal oxide precursor aerogel is a three-dimensional structure, wherein drying the boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel results in shrinking the three-dimensional structure to a size having an average diameter in a range of greater than 0% and less than 5% of an average diameter of the three-dimensional structure before drying. 20. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein the drying comprises drying by an evaporation process under ambient conditions, wherein a temperature of the drying is room temperature, wherein the dried boron @ metal oxide precursor aerogel is a boron @ metal oxide xerogel. 21. The method as recited in claim 10, wherein the heating of the boron@ metal oxide aerogel comprises: heating to a first temperature sufficient to effect a bromothermal reduction reaction; and 

 heating to a second temperature sufficient to effect a thermal annealing reaction. 22. The method as recited in claim 21, wherein the first temperature is in a range of about 600Â°C to about 1400Â°C. 23. The method as recited in claim 21, wherein the second temperature is in a range of about 1400Â°C to about 2000Â°C.
